task: I may need to step out during part of my shift later this week, so I’m putting together a quick note for the person who might cover for me. Can you help me make it clear what needs to be done and what they should know before taking over?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — send me the details, and I’ll turn them into a clear handoff note with:
- what needs to be done
- what’s already been handled
- what they should watch for
- anything urgent or time-sensitive

If you want, I can keep it short and plain so it’s easy to read fast.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. It’s for housekeeping, morning shift. I’d want it to say the carts are stocked, room list is at the desk, and any rooms with do-not-disturb or late checkout need checked again before you skip them. If there’s a maintenance issue, write it down right away and tell the front desk — don’t just leave it. Also mention to watch for wet carpet by the ice machine, and if a room looks off, check the bathroom and under the beds before marking it clean. Keep it plain. Something like:

"I may have to step out during part of my shift later this week. If you’re covering, please check the room list with the front desk first, keep an eye on any late checkouts or do-not-disturb rooms, and make sure carts stay stocked. Report any maintenance problems right away and write them down clearly. If anything seems out of place, double-check the bathroom, bed linens, and carpet before marking the room finished."

That’s the kind of thing.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a plain version you can hand off:

**If I have to step out during part of my shift, please check the room list with the front desk first. Keep an eye on late checkouts and do-not-disturb rooms before skipping them. Make sure the carts stay stocked. Write down and report any maintenance problems right away. If a room looks off, check the bathroom, bed linens, and carpet before marking it clean. Also watch the carpet by the ice machine for any wet spots.**

If you want, I can make it even shorter and more like a quick note card.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Plain enough, and it says what needs saying without carrying on.
